hypothesis: For lanthanide-based metal superhydrides (specifically La and Ce) at pressures between 150 and 250 GPa, introducing non-integer hydrogen-to-metal ratios in the range 9.5–10.5 yields hydrogen cage motifs that increase the electron–phonon coupling constant λ by at least 10% and raise the superconducting Tc by at least 10 K compared to the corresponding integer MH10 phases under identical conditions.
